DECLARATION OF EVELYN L. DEATON IN SUPPORT OF DEFENTDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 15342051) FILED BY DEFENDANT PARKING SYSTEMS OF AMERICA, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT PARKING SYSTEMS OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1281 AND TO STAY THE ACTION. A PARTY WHO RESISTS ARBITRATION ON THE GROUND OF WAIVER BEARS A HEAVY BURDEN AND ANY DOUBTS REGARDING A WAIVER ALLEGATION SHOULD BE RESOLVED IN FAVOR OF ARBITRATION. (ST. AGNES MEDICAL CENTER V. PACIFICARE (2003) 31 CAL.4TH 1187.) SOBREMEONTE V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 61 CAL.APP.4TH 980, 992 SETS OUT THE FACTORS WHICH THE COURT MAY CONSIDER IN DETERMINING WHETHER THE PARTY WAIVED ITS RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. HERE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN IN ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT WAIVED ITS RIGHT TO ARBITRATE THE MATTER. THE ONLY DEMAND MADE IN THE INSTANT CASE OCCURRED ON SEPTEMBER 8, 2015. HERE, THERE IS NOT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF MADE REPEATED REQUESTS FOR DEFENDANT TO ARBITRATE. (SEE LOCAL 659, I.A.T.S.E. V. COLOR CORP. OF AMER. (1956) 47 CAL.2D 189, 302 P.2D 294 [WAIVER AND REPUDIATION FOUND WHERE PLAINTIFF REFUSED DEFENDANT'S REPEATED DEMANDS TO COMPLY WITH A CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION CLAUSE].) THE ADDITIONAL FACTORS IDENTIFIED BY THE COURT IN SOBREMEONTE V. SUPERIOR COURT ARE NOT PRESENT. THE LITIGATION MACHINERY HAS NOT BEEN SUBSTANTIALLY INVOKED, IMPORTANT INTERVENING STEPS [E.G., TAKING ADVANTAGE OF JUDICIAL DISCOVERY PROCEDURES NOT AVAILABLE IN ARBITRATION] HAVE NOT TAKEN PLACE, AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ARGUMENT OR EVIDENCE WHICH SUGGESTS THAT THE DELAY HAS 'AFFECTED, MISLED, OR PREJUDICED' PLAINTIFF. (SOBREMEONTE 61 CAL.APP.4TH 980, 992.) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-02-2016 CONTINUED TO MAY-04-2016 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR DEFENDANT(S) TO FILE ORDER RE ARBITRATION AND STAY. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Continued To May-04-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Jan-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PARKING SYSTEMS OF AMERICA, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is granted. The court grants the motion to compel arbitration pursuant to CCP section 1281 and to stay the action. A party who resists arbitration on the ground of waiver bears a heavy burden and any doubts regarding a waiver allegation should be resolved in favor of arbitration. (St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187.) Sobremeonte v. Superior Court (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 980, 992 sets out the factors which the court may consider in determining whether the party waived its right to arbitrate. Here, Plaintiff has not met its burden in establishing that Defendant waived its right to arbitrate the matter. The only demand made in the instant case occurred on September 8, 2015. Here, there is not evidence that Plaintiff made repeated requests for Defendant to arbitrate. (See Local 659, I.A.T.S.E. v. Color Corp. of Amer. (1956) 47 Cal.2d 189, 302 P.2d 294 [waiver and repudiation found where plaintiff refused defendant's repeated demands to comply with a contractual arbitration clause].) The additional factors identified by the court in Sobremeonte v. Superior Court are not present. The litigation machinery has not been substantially invoked, important intervening steps [e.g., taking advantage of judicial discovery procedures not available in arbitration] have not taken place, and Plaintiff has not presented argument or evidence which suggests that the delay has 'affected, misled, or prejudiced' Plaintiff. (Sobremeonte 61 Cal.App.4th 980, 992.) The prevailing party is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
